questions.question: When you signed with the agency, did anyone walk you through the contract line by line — or did you sign it mostly on your own?
questions.answer: I signed it after reading through it on my own

questions.question: Did you know your contract automatically renewed in April 2026 for another full year? Were you aware that was coming?
questions.answer: Yes I did

questions.question: In your own words — what do you think would happen if you wanted to leave the agency before April 2027?
questions.answer: I think I would have to pay a fee for leaving but it’s also on their terms if I can recall correctly

questions.question: When you get paid for a brand deal, what percentage do you actually receive? What percentage goes to the agency?
questions.answer: 73% to me, 25 to the agency

questions.question: Your contract mentions a "Talent Manager" who receives 10% of your earnings. Do you know who that is and what they do for you?
questions.answer: Yes my talent manager is Warens who is the founder of Wawa productions, he is the one who is filtering through brand deals for me

questions.question: Does the agency take a cut of your TikTok LIVE income — the gifts and subscriptions your fans send you directly? Or is that money fully yours?
questions.answer: That money is fully mine, TikTok however takes their own cut

questions.question: When a brand pays for a deal — does the money come to you directly, or does it go to the agency first and then they pay you?
questions.answer: It goes through the agency first then to me

questions.question: Are you currently paying the agency an annual fee of $250? Have you been charged this?
questions.answer: I have not

questions.question: Roughly how much have you earned through the agency in total since signing? And how much do you feel you've spent on business-related costs (costumes, equipment, travel, etc.)?
questions.answer: In Wawa productions I believe around $400, from Wawa live it’s currently up towards 1.1k within the past 3 months

questions.question: Where did the 65-hour streaming requirement come from? Was it something you agreed to in writing, or was it a verbal conversation?
questions.answer: This was a campaign through tiktok where the requirement is all on my own accord if I want to complete it for the opportunities that the campaign offers. It was a “sign up” that I started

questions.question: What platform does the 65 hours apply to — TikTok LIVE only, or other platforms too? How do you currently track it?
questions.answer: TikTok live only, it is tracked on TikTok itself on the campaigns tab

questions.question: What happens if you don't hit 65 hours in a given month? Has anyone told you what the consequence is?
questions.answer: I don’t qualify for the opportunities that campaign offers, it’s a you hit it or you don’t situation

questions.question: How many brand deals has the agency brought you in the past 6 months? Can you name them?
questions.answer: 1 song promotion

questions.question: Have any brands reached out to you directly — through DMs, email, or at events? If so, what did you do with those inquiries?
questions.answer: I send them over to my agency’s email contact for me

questions.question: Have you done any deals, collabs, appearances, or paid posts that the agency wasn't involved in or didn't know about?
questions.answer: No paid collaborations

questions.question: Have you ever signed something the agency sent over without reading it fully first?
questions.answer: No I haven’t

questions.question: How often does someone from the agency actually reach out to you — weekly, monthly, only when there's a deal?
questions.answer: Typically it’s weekly / bi weekly when it’s related to Wawa productions but when it’s related to Wawa live, there has been a weekly communication

questions.question: Did you know that conventions and public appearances technically have to be reviewed and cleared by the agency first? Have you been doing that?
questions.answer: I have not

questions.question: Have you attended any conventions, events, or meetups without running it through the agency first?
questions.answer: Yes, a few

questions.question: Do you have any ongoing brand relationships or partnerships that the agency didn't set up — brands you connected with on your own?
questions.answer: Yes, however that company hasn’t done any deals with me recently

questions.question: What do you think it would take to leave the agency? What's your understanding of how that process works?
questions.answer: I believe there’s a fee for leaving

questions.question: Did you know that for 12 months after leaving the agency, you legally cannot independently work with any brand they introduced you to — without their written permission?
questions.answer: Yes

questions.question: Did you know that any disputes with the agency would be handled under Canadian law in Quebec — not in Washington State where you live?
questions.answer: Yes

questions.question: Overall — how do you feel about your relationship with the agency right now? Is it working for you?
questions.answer: It’s not working the way I want it to but I also know that’s because I haven’t been branding myself for those opportunities to arise. I haven’t had the time to create more solo content because lack of time
